evals.title: ServiceChannel one-per-object-type constraint
evals.prompt: Can I use the same ServiceChannel to route both Case and LiveChatTranscript records in Salesforce OmniChannel?
expected_behaviors: States that one ServiceChannel maps to exactly one SObject type via RelatedEntityType
Explains that Case and LiveChatTranscript require separate ServiceChannel records
Mentions that ServiceChannel.DeveloperName is immutable after creation
Does not suggest workarounds that use a single channel for multiple object types

evals.title: RoutingModel push vs pull distinction
evals.prompt: What is the difference between MostAvailable and LeastActive routing models in Salesforce OmniChannel, and which is push vs pull?
expected_behaviors: Identifies both MostAvailable and LeastActive as push routing models
Explains MostAvailable routes to the agent with the most remaining capacity units
Explains LeastActive routes to the agent with the fewest active work items by count
Clarifies that push means work is sent to agents without requiring them to pull from a queue
Mentions ExternalRouting as the third RoutingModel option

evals.title: AgentWork status lifecycle correctness
evals.prompt: What are the valid AgentWork status transitions in Salesforce OmniChannel? Can I set Status = Closed directly without going through Accepted?
expected_behaviors: Lists the full lifecycle: Assigned, Opened, Accepted, Declined, Closed
Explains that Assigned is set by the routing engine when work is first routed
Explains that Declined triggers re-routing via a new PendingServiceRouting record
Warns that setting Status = Closed programmatically without closing the underlying work item causes orphaned records that inflate supervisor metrics
Notes that the platform closes AgentWork automatically when the work item (Case, Chat) is closed

evals.title: Capacity units vs work item count
evals.prompt: An OmniChannel agent has a ConfiguredCapacity of 10. They have two active Case work items. Are they at capacity? Explain how OmniChannel capacity works.
expected_behaviors: Explains that capacity is measured in capacity units, not raw work item count
States that RoutingConfiguration.Capacity (or Units of Capacity) sets how many units each work item consumes
Explains that with two Case items each consuming 5 units, the agent would be at 10/10 = 100% capacity
Clarifies that with two Case items each consuming 2 units, the agent would be at 4/10 = 40% capacity and can receive more work
Does not confuse capacity units with work item count

evals.title: PresenceStatus channel linkage required for routing
evals.prompt: We created a new PresenceStatus called 'Available - Cases' but agents in that status are not receiving any routed cases. What is the most likely cause?
expected_behaviors: Identifies that PresenceStatus must have ServiceChannels linked under Presence Status Channels
Explains that a PresenceStatus without linked ServiceChannels never routes work to agents in that status
Describes the fix: edit the PresenceStatus in Setup and add the Case ServiceChannel to the Service Channels list
Does not suggest the issue is with the RoutingConfiguration or queue first without confirming channel linkage

evals.title: Skills routing multi-object chain requirement
evals.prompt: I set up a RoutingAttributeDefinition for 'Language' and assigned agents the skill via AgentCapacityOverride, but cases are still routing to agents without the Spanish language skill. What am I missing?
expected_behaviors: Identifies that RoutingAttribute must be created on the RoutingConfiguration linking it to the RoutingAttributeDefinition
Explains the full chain: RoutingAttributeDefinition → RoutingAttribute (on RoutingConfiguration) → AgentCapacityOverride
Notes that a missing RoutingAttribute causes silent fallback to queue routing with no error
May also mention AgentWorkSkill if per-work-item skill requirements are needed
Recommends verifying the full chain in sandbox with SOQL queries

evals.title: Do not delete PendingServiceRouting records
evals.prompt: There are old PendingServiceRouting records in our org piling up. Can I delete them via Data Loader to clean them up?
expected_behaviors: Clearly states that deleting PendingServiceRouting records manually abandons the work items silently
Explains that after deletion the customer's Case or Chat remains open but no agent will ever be assigned
Recommends resolving the root cause instead: add more agents, fix skill coverage, or check agent presence status
Suggests setting AgentWork.Status = Declined as the correct way to re-queue stuck work
Does not recommend deletion as a valid cleanup approach

evals.title: QueueSobject required for queue-based OmniChannel routing
evals.prompt: I created a queue for Cases and associated it with a RoutingConfiguration, but OmniChannel is not routing Cases from the queue to agents. What is missing?
expected_behaviors: Identifies that QueueSobject records must link the queue to the Case SObject type
Explains that creating a queue without adding Case under Supported Objects means no QueueSobject exists for Case
Provides the SOQL to verify: SELECT Id, QueueId, SobjectType FROM QueueSobject WHERE QueueId = '<id>'
States that a queue without the correct QueueSobject will not receive Case routing from OmniChannel even if the RoutingConfiguration is correctly configured

evals.title: Unified Routing add-on license prerequisite
evals.prompt: We enabled Unified Routing in Setup but cases are still not being routed through the Unified Routing engine. What prerequisites are we missing?
expected_behaviors: States that Unified Routing requires either the Messaging for In-App and Web or Digital Engagement add-on license
Explains that enabling Unified Routing without the add-on license shows the UI but does not activate the routing engine
May mention that each ServiceChannel must be individually opted into Unified Routing via IsEnabled for Unified Routing = true
Does not suggest Unified Routing works with a base Service Cloud license alone

evals.title: AgentWork query filter required in supervisor context
evals.prompt: Write a SOQL query to get all active OmniChannel work items for a supervisor dashboard. What filters are essential to avoid governor limit issues?
expected_behaviors: Includes WHERE Status NOT IN ('Closed', 'Declined') filter
Recommends adding CreatedDate = TODAY or a recent date range for high-volume orgs
Explains that unfiltered AgentWork queries can return millions of historical rows and cause governor limit failures
Does not provide a SOQL query with SELECT * FROM AgentWork with no WHERE clause

evals.title: Do not manually insert AgentWork records
evals.prompt: I want to test OmniChannel routing in my sandbox by programmatically creating AgentWork records in Apex to simulate routing. Is this a valid approach?
expected_behaviors: States clearly that manually inserting AgentWork records is not a valid approach
Explains that manual inserts bypass the routing engine's capacity and availability checks
Explains the consequence: ghost work items that appear in supervisor view but don't reflect real routing state
Recommends using OmniRouterService Apex class or the assignWorkToAgent Connect API for programmatic routing
Recommends using actual test agents with proper presence status for sandbox testing

evals.title: UserServicePresence.ConfiguredCapacity is fixed at setup
evals.prompt: We want to give agents different capacity limits on Monday vs Friday based on expected volume. Can we update UserServicePresence.ConfiguredCapacity dynamically each day?
expected_behaviors: States that ConfiguredCapacity is set at the ServicePresenceConfig (Presence Configuration) level, not per-shift or per-day
Explains that updating UserServicePresence.ConfiguredCapacity directly via Apex or Data Loader has no effect on routing
Recommends using AgentCapacityOverride records for per-agent capacity exceptions
May suggest creating separate Presence Configurations for different capacity tiers and assigning agents to them as needed
Does not suggest that ConfiguredCapacity can be dynamically changed per session
